reports.name: deltaR_matching_study.pdf
reports.title: Study of the ╬ФR Truth-Matching Requirement in Single-Interaction MC
reports.text: sPHENIX Internal Note Study of the тИЖR Truth-Matching Requirement in Single-Interaction MC Isolated Photon Cross-Section in p+p at тИЪs = 200 GeV sPHENIX Collaboration June 2, 2026 Abstract This note documents a study of the тИЖR < 0.1 geometric truth-matching requirement used in the reconstruction eямГciency calculation for the sPHENIX isolated photon cross-section mea- surement (PPG12). While a previous study of double-interaction MC found a 65% matching failure rate driven by vertex displacement, the single-interaction samples were assumed to be unaямАected. We show that even in single-interaction MC, the тИЖR cut rejects тИ╝3.5% of correctly track-ID-matched truth photons, with the rejection driven entirely by vertex mismatch rather than cluster quality. 73% of rejected clusters have ET/ptruth T in the range [0.8, 1.2] and are well- reconstructed photons. The тИЖR < 0.1 cut is removed from the production eямГciency calculation; track ID matching alone provides the deямБnitive truth association. Contents 1 Introduction 2 2 Method 2 3 Results 3 3.1 тИЖR Failure Rates . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 3 3.2 Threshold Scan . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 3 3.3 Vertex Mismatch as the Driving Mechanism . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 3 3.4 Energy Quality of Rejected Clusters . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 6 3.5 Extension to Double-Interaction MC: тИЖ╬╖ vs. тИЖz . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . 9 4 Physics Argument for Removing the тИЖR Cut 9 5 Conclusion 10 1 1 Introduction The reconstruction eямГciency for the sPHENIX isolated photon cross-section measurement is com- puted from single-interaction PYTHIA + GEANT4 MC samples. A truth photon is considered тАЬmatchedтАЭ to a reconstructed cluster if two criteria are satisямБed: 1. Track ID match: the GEANT track ID (trkID) of the truth particle matches that of a reconstructed cluster. This is the deямБnitive truth association provided by the simulation framework. 2. Geometric match: the angular separation between the truth photon and the matched cluster satisямБes тИЖR = p (тИЖ╬╖)2 + (тИЖ╧Ж)2 < 0.1. The тИЖR cut was introduced as a geometric sanity check on top of the trkID association. A previous study of double-interaction MC (documented in Ref. [1]) demonstrated that vertex dis- placement causes a 65% тИЖR-matching failure rate. In double-interaction events, the reconstructed vertex is the average of two collision vertices, inducing large тИЖz shifts that propagate to тИЖ╬╖ via the EMCal geometry. That study focused on double-interaction samples, and single-interaction MC was assumed to be largely unaямАected. This note quantiямБes the тИЖR cutтАЩs impact in single-interaction MC and evaluates whether it is needed at all. References [1] sPHENIX Internal Note: Double-Interaction MC EямГciency Study, 2025. 2 Method The study uses the macro study deltaR single.C, processing the full single-interaction MC sam- ples used in the production eямГciency calculation: photon5 (truth pT range 0тАУ14 GeV), photon10 (14тАУ30 GeV), and photon20 (30+ GeV). Each sample is weighted by its production cross-section to form a combined spectrum spanning the analysis range 8 тЙдpT < 36 GeV. Truth photon selection follows the standard PPG12 criteria: тАв Particle ID: pid = 22 тАв Photon class: photonclass < 3 (direct photons) тАв Truth isolation: Eiso,truth T < 4 GeV (cone Rcone = 0.3) тАв Pseudorapidity: |╬╖| < 0.7 тАв Transverse momentum: 8 тЙдpT < 36 GeV For each truth photon, the closest trkID-matched reconstructed cluster is identiямБed, and тИЖR is computed. The analysis scans over тИЖR thresholds of 0.05, 0.08, 0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.30, and no geometric cut (track ID only). The photon10 double sample is also processed for direct comparison with the double-interaction results. 2 3 Results 3.1 тИЖR Failure Rates Table 1 summarizes the matching failure rates per sample and for the combined (unweighted) dataset. Table 1: Truth-matching failure rates in single-interaction MC, per sample and combined. тАЬNo trkID matchтАЭ indicates no reconstructed cluster carries the truth photonтАЩs track ID. тАЬFail тИЖR < 0.1тАЭ is the fraction of trkID-matched photons that fail the geometric cut. Sample Truth photons No trkID match Fail тИЖR < 0.1 (of matched) photon5 273,017 3.42% 2.70% photon10 2,380,500 2.37% 2.98% photon20 2,840,703 1.72% 4.01% Combined 5,494,220 2.09% 3.50% The тИЖR < 0.1 cut rejects 2.7тАУ4.0% of truth photons that already have a valid trkID match, with the rate increasing at higher pT where forward-╬╖ clusters are more sensitive to vertex shifts. Table 2 compares the single-interaction and double-interaction results for the photon10 sample, illustrating the continuity between the two regimes. Table 2: Comparison of photon10 single-interaction and double-interaction MC. The тИЖz RMS is the event-level distribution of |zreco vtx тИТztruth vtx |. Sample тИЖz RMS No trkID Fail тИЖR < 0.1 (of matched) photon10 single 9.8 cm 2.37% 3.06% photon10 double 46.2 cm 7.92% 65.0% The тИЖR failure rate scales with vertex displacement: the double-interaction sampleтАЩs much larger тИЖz RMS drives a proportionally larger failure rate, consistent with the geometric mechanism established in the double-interaction study. 3.2 Threshold Scan Table 3 shows the тИЖR failure rate as a function of the matching threshold for both single and double-interaction photon10 samples. Even relaxing the cut to тИЖR < 0.30 still rejects 1.3% of single-interaction photons, indicating that the issue is not a matter of tuning the threshold but reямВects the fundamental limitation of a geometric cut applied on top of a vertex-dependent coordinate system. 3.3 Vertex Mismatch as the Driving Mechanism The connection between vertex mismatch and тИЖR failure is examined by computing the failure fraction as a function of |тИЖz| = |zreco vtx тИТztruth vtx |. (1). 3.4 Energy Quality of Rejected Clusters If the тИЖR cut were removing genuinely mis-reconstructed clusters, we would expect degraded energy response. Figure 6 shows the cluster ET/ptruth T distribution separately for clusters that pass and fail the тИЖR < 0.1 cut. The majority of rejected clusters are well-reconstructed photons. Their calorimeter energy deposit is correct; only the angular position is shifted by the vertex mismatch. The modest reduction in mean ET/ptruth T for failing clusters (0.899 vs. 0.945) is itself a consequence of the ╬╖-shift: the ET = E/ cosh ╬╖ calculation uses the displaced vertex, producing a slightly diямАerent ET than the truth-level pT. 6 0 200 400 600 800 1000 1200 1400 1600 1800 2000 2200 2400 6 10 ├Ч 30 тИТ 20 тИТ 10 тИТ 0 10 20 30 [cm] truth vtx тИТ reco = vtx dz 0.15 тИТ 0.1 тИТ 0.05 тИТ 0 0.05 0.1 0.15 truth) тИТ (cluster ╬╖ тИЖ = 0 approx.) ╬╖ ( CEMC R / dz тИТ Internal sPHENIX = 200 GeV s p + p | < 0.7 ╬│ ╬╖ | Figure 4: Correlation between тИЖ╬╖ (truthтИТreco) and vertex shift тИЖz. Figure 7 compares the тИЖ╬╖ vs. тИЖz correlation for photon10 single-interaction (left) and photon10 double full GEANT double-interaction MC (right), using 2M events per sample with the standard truth photon selection (pid = 22, photonclass < 3, Eiso,truth T < 4 GeV, |╬╖| < 0.7, 8 тЙдpT < 36 GeV). In double-interaction events, the MBD-reconstructed vertex is approximately the average of two collision vertices: zreco vtx тЙИz1 + z2 2 , тИЖz тЙИz2 тИТz1 2 (2) where z1 is the primary (hard photon) collision vertex and z2 is the pileup collision vertex. This broadens the event-level тИЖz distribution from an RMS of 16.9 cm (single, before vertex cut) to 48.1 cm (double), and correspondingly the cluster тИЖ╬╖ distribution from RMS 0.073 to 0.365. Both distributions follow the geometric prediction тИЖ╬╖ тЙИтИТтИЖz/RCEMC (red dashed line), con- ямБrming that the ╬╖-shift mechanism is identical in both regimes тАФ only the magnitude of the vertex displacement diямАers. Table 6: Summary of vertex displacement and matching statistics for photon10 single and double- interaction MC (2M events each, trkID-matched truth photons within |zreco vtx | < 60 cm). Metric Single Double Event-level тИЖz RMS (all events) 16.9 cm 48.1 cm Cluster тИЖ╬╖ RMS (matched) 0.073 0.365 No trkID match 2.33% 7.95% Fail тИЖR < 0.1 (of matched) 3.04% 65.08% Events with |тИЖz| > 9.35 cm 36.0% 74.2% The practical consequence: 65.1% of trkID-matched clusters in double-interaction MC fail the тИЖR < 0.1 cut (vs. 3.0% in single), and 74.2% of events exceed the critical |тИЖz| = 9.35 cm threshold. This provides further quantitative support for removing the geometric matching requirement. 4 Physics Argument for Removing the тИЖR Cut Six arguments support removing the тИЖR < 0.1 requirement: 1. Track ID is ground truth. The GEANT trkID provides a deямБnitive link between the truth particle and its energy deposit in the calorimeter. The тИЖR cut adds no additional truth information; it tests a derived geometric quantity that depends on the vertex reconstruction. 2. The cut tests vertex resolution, not calorimeter performance. The failure rate correlates with |тИЖz| (Table 4), not with any measure of cluster quality. The sharp step at |тИЖz| = 9.35 cm is a geometric artifact of the EMCal radius and the тИЖR threshold. 3. Rejected clusters are good photons. 73% of failing clusters have ET/ptruth T тИИ[0.8, 1.2] (Table 5). The calorimeter correctly measured the photon energy; only the vertex-dependent angular coordinate is shifted. 9 1 10 2 10 3 10 4 10 5 10 30 тИТ 20 тИТ 10 тИТ 0 10 20 30 [cm] truth vtx тИТ reco = vtx dz 0.15 тИТ 0.1 тИТ 0.05 тИТ 0 0.05 0.1 0.15 truth) тИТ (cluster ╬╖ тИЖ CEMC R / dz тИТ Internal sPHENIX Single interaction photon10 MC 1 10 2 10 3 10 4 10 100 тИТ 50 тИТ 0 50 100 [cm] truth vtx тИТ reco = vtx dz 1 тИТ 0.5 тИТ 0 0.5 1 truth) тИТ (cluster ╬╖ тИЖ CEMC R / dz тИТ Internal sPHENIX Double interaction photon10_double MC Figure 7: Correlation between тИЖ╬╖ (cluster тИТtruth) and vertex displacement тИЖz = zreco vtx тИТztruth vtx for photon10 single-interaction MC (left) and full GEANT double-interaction MC (right). The red dashed line shows the geometric prediction тИЖ╬╖ = тИТтИЖz/RCEMC at ╬╖ = 0. Note the diямАerent axis scales: the double-interaction тИЖz extends to ┬▒120 cm (vs. ┬▒30 cm for single) and тИЖ╬╖ to ┬▒1.2 (vs. ┬▒0.15). 4. The response matrix should capture vertex-induced shifts. Kinematic migration from vertex mismatch (the slight ET shift visible in the failing clusters) is precisely the kind of detector eямАect that the unfolding response matrix is designed to correct. Excluding these events from the eямГciency numerator biases the response matrix. 5. The тИ╝3% bias is vertex-dependent. Because the failure rate depends on |тИЖz|, it is correlated with beam conditions (vertex spread, crossing angle). An eямГciency correction that includes this bias would need to be parameterized in тИЖz, adding unnecessary complexity. 6. Consistency between single and double MC. Removing the тИЖR cut makes the single- interaction and double-interaction eямГciency calculations more directly comparable, simplify- ing the pileup systematic uncertainty evaluation. We note that the production code contains a commented-out alternative cut, (cluster ET / particle Pt) > 0.8, which directly tests calorimeter energy response rather than angular ge- ometry. If any quality gate beyond trkID is desired, an energy-response cut is more physically motivated than a geometric тИЖR cut, as it removes genuinely mis-reconstructed clusters (merges, splits, lost energy) without penalizing vertex displacement. 5 Conclusion The тИЖR < 0.1 geometric truth-matching cut is removed from the production reconstruction eямГ- ciency calculation. The key ямБndings are: 1. In single-interaction MC, the тИЖR < 0.1 cut rejects тИ╝3.5% of trkID-matched truth photons. This is not negligible at the precision targets of the measurement. 10 2. The rejection is driven entirely by vertex mismatch (|тИЖz| > 9.35 cm), not by cluster quality. A sharp step function in failure rate vs. |тИЖz| matches the geometric prediction from the EMCal barrel radius. 3. 73% of rejected clusters are well-reconstructed photons with ET/ptruth T тИИ[0.8, 1.2]. 4. Track ID matching alone is suямГcient and deямБnitive for truth association. The тИЖR cut adds no truth information and introduces a vertex-dependent eямГciency bias. 5. The тИ╝3% diямАerence in eямГciency can be assigned as a systematic uncertainty if desired, though the physics argument supports treating the track-ID-only matching as the correct baseline. 6. The тИЖ╬╖ vs. тИЖz correlation in full GEANT double-interaction MC (Section 3.5) conямБrms that the same geometric mechanism operates at larger vertex displacements (тИЖz RMS 48.1 cm vs. 16.9 cm), with 65% of matched clusters exceeding the тИЖR = 0.1 threshold. 11

reports.text: Topo-cluster isolation distributions and photon-energy containment vs inner-R PPG12 isolated photon analysis April 19, 2026 1 Motivation The nominal PPG12 isolation variable is the R = 0.4 topo-cluster energy around the seed cluster: recoisonom ET = cluster iso topo 04, which by construction in CaloAna24 equals ╬гR<0.4 Etopo T тИТEseed T . To study an тАЬannulusтАЭ variant we subtract an inner ring, parametrised by a new conямБguration key iso topo innerr: recoisoann ET (Rin) = cluster iso topo 04 тИТcluster iso topoRin. Because both terms have a common тИТEseed T , the subtraction amounts to the sum in the annulus R тИИ[Rin, 0.4]. The question addressed here is how much of the photonтАЩs own energy sits outside a given inner radius Rin, and how the one-dimensional isolation distribution evolves with R in truth-matched signal MC, inclusive MC, and data. 2 Samples and selection тАв Truth-matched signal MC: photon5 + photon10 + photon20, cross-section weighted. For a reco cluster to enter the study, its cluster truthtrkID must map to a truth particle with pid = 22, photonclass = 1 (direct), and particle truth iso 03 < 4 GeV (ямБducial). тАв Inclusive MC: photon5/10/20 + jet5/8/12/20/30/40, cross-section weighted, no truth match (all reconstructed clusters). тАв Data: 77 part * with bdt split.root ямБles from the ana521 production, capped at 5├Ч105 clusters for this study (reduced from 3├Ч106 in the earlier pass тАФ see revision note in ┬з4). тАв Pre-common: no cluster-quality selection beyond the sample ямБlter. тАв Post-common: require cluster npb score > 0.5 (the only active common cut in config bdt nom.yaml, since wr cogx bound = 0 and cluster weta cogx bound = 2 disable the shape cuts). Inner-R branches scanned: Rin тИИ{0.05, 0.075, 0.10, 0.20, 0.30, 0.40}. 3 Isolation distributions vs inner radius Figure 1 overlays the six cluster iso topoR distributions on each of the three samples. Figure 6 compares, per R, the data and inclusive MC distributions. Table 1 summarises the post-common mean and RMS. 1 20 тИТ 15 тИТ 10 тИТ 5 тИТ 0 5 10 15 20 25 30 [GeV] R topo iso 4 тИТ 10 3 тИТ 10 2 тИТ 10 1 тИТ 10 1 Normalised R = 0.05 R = 0.075 R = 0.1 R = 0.2 R = 0.3 R = 0.4 Internal sPHENIX ╬│ MC truth-matched direct post-common 20 тИТ 15 тИТ 10 тИТ 5 тИТ 0 5 10 15 20 25 30 [GeV] R topo iso 4 тИТ 10 3 тИТ 10 2 тИТ 10 1 тИТ 10 1 Normalised R = 0.05 R = 0.075 R = 0.1 R = 0.2 R = 0.3 R = 0.4 Inclusive MC post-common 20 тИТ 15 тИТ 10 тИТ 5 тИТ 0 5 10 15 20 25 30 [GeV] R topo iso 4 тИТ 10 3 тИТ 10 2 тИТ 10 1 тИТ 10 1 Normalised R = 0.05 R = 0.075 R = 0.1 R = 0.2 R = 0.3 R = 0.4 Data post-common Figure 1: cluster iso topoR distributions (log-y, unit-normalised) for the six inner radii, on truth- matched signal MC (left), inclusive MC (middle) and data (right), all post-common. Inclusive in cluster ET . 3.1 Per-cluster-ET expansion (linear y) Figure 2 to 5 repeat Figure 1 in four cluster-ET windows with linear y-scale to make the peak structure visible. All samples and the six inner radii follow the same colour assignment; post- common selection applies throughout. 20 тИТ 15 тИТ 10 тИТ 5 тИТ 0 5 10 15 20 25 30 [GeV] R topo iso 0 0.2 0.4 0.6 0.8 1 1.2 Normalised R = 0.05 R = 0.075 R = 0.1 R = 0.2 R = 0.3 R = 0.4 Internal sPHENIX MC ╬│ Truth-matched [8, 14] GeV тИИ T E 20 тИТ 15 тИТ 10 тИТ 5 тИТ 0 5 10 15 20 25 30 [GeV] R topo iso 0 0.2 0.4 0.6 0.8 1 Normalised R = 0.05 R = 0.075 R = 0.1 R = 0.2 R = 0.3 R = 0.4 Inclusive MC [8, 14] GeV тИИ T E 20 тИТ 15 тИТ 10 тИТ 5 тИТ 0 5 10 15 20 25 30 [GeV] R topo iso 0 0.2 0.4 0.6 0.8 1 Normalised R = 0.05 R = 0.075 R = 0.1 R = 0.2 R = 0.3 R = 0.4 Data [8, 14] GeV тИИ T E Figure 2: Cluster ET тИИ[8, 14] GeV, linear y. Observations. With the current slimtreeтАЩs seed-subtracted cluster iso topoR deямБnition, the truth-matched MC sits essentially at zero across R (mean 0.07 тЖТ0.29 GeV, RMS 0.64тАУ0.76 GeV), the photon energy being cleanly removed by the explicit тИТEseed T term. Inclusive MC and data track one another in shape but diverge mildly in the tails: inclusive-MC means are тЙИ0.3тАУ0.7 GeV above data at R тЙе0.2, and the inclusive-MC RMS is тЙИ15тАУ20% larger than data at R = 0.4. This is consistent with the MC mix being jet-richer than the as-taken data (which sits post-common after the NPB > 0.5 shower-shape cut). 4 Tower-based isolation: same study, per cluster ET For completeness the same per-cluster-ET overlay is repeated with the tower-based (cluster isoR) branches, which sum EMCAL + HCALIN + HCALOUT tower energy within R of the seed. Revision note. The extraction was re-run with a reduced sample for iteration speed on slow GPFS: 3├Ч105 clusters per inclusive-MC sample and 5├Ч105 data clusters, vs. 2├Ч106 and 3├Ч106 respectively in the earlier pass. Containment reaches тИ╝95% at R = 0.10 and saturates above unity by R = 0.20 (the slight excess over 1.0 reямВects residual background plus cluster-shape calibration details).
